title: Run stakes — completion tracking in careerStats + bag-full feedback
problem: Two gaps: (1) Stake completions are never recorded in careerStats, so there is no history of which stakes were completed and no basis for future meta-unlocks tied to stake achievements. (2) When the bag is full and a stake bonus item would drop, it is silently discarded — the player gets no notification and may not realize they missed a reward.

lineage.spawnReason: bug + deferred tracking: silent bag-full drop is a UX bug; careerStats tracking was omitted from the initial stakes implementation

analysis: Two independent gaps identified during feat-2026-058 frozen review. Gap 1 (tracking): claimStake() currently sets stakeClaimed=true and maybe sets stakeBonusItem, but never writes to careerStats. There is no record of how many stakes a player has completed, nor which stake types. This blocks any future achievement system or cross-run stake feedback. The fix is a careerStats.stakesCompleted: Record<StakeId, number> counter incremented on claim. Gap 2 (bag-full UX): GridScreen's useEffect that delivers the bonus item calls inventoryStore.addItem() — if it returns false (bag full), stakeBonusItem is cleared silently. Players miss the reward with no indication. The fix: show a Toast when the add fails, and optionally offer to deposit to stash.
knownLimitations: stakesCompleted is not displayed in any UI yet — it exists as data for future achievement screens
If activeStake is null when claimStake fires (edge case), it is recorded under key 'none'
implementation: 1. gameStore.ts: added stakesCompleted: Record<string, number> to CareerStats interface and default initial state. hydrate() includes stakesCompleted: data.careerStats?.stakesCompleted ?? {}. claimStake() now reads activeStake, builds updatedStakesCompleted by incrementing the count for that stake key, and writes it to careerStats alongside stakeClaimed. Added scheduleSave() call to claimStake() (was missing). 2. GridScreen.tsx: stake bonus item useEffect now checks addItem() return value. If false (bag full), calls depositToStash() as fallback. If that also returns false (stash full), shows a 'bag & stash full, bonus item lost!' warning toast. Toast duration extended to 4500ms for the bag-full variant.

decisions.decision: stakesCompleted keyed by StakeId string in careerStats, not a separate top-level field
decisions.rationale: careerStats is the canonical cross-run aggregate. Keeping stake counts there makes them available alongside totalRuns, deepestFloor etc. for any future trophy/achievement UI.

decisions.decision: Bag-full fallback deposits to stash before warning about loss
decisions.rationale: Silent item loss is a worse experience than stash overflow. The player can clear stash next town visit. Only if both are full do we warn about loss — an extreme edge case.

successCriteria: After claiming a stake, careerStats.stakesCompleted[stakeId] is incremented and persisted. Stake bonus item delivery attempts bag first, stash second, then shows loss warning. No silent item drops.
userGuide: Stakes are tracked permanently across runs. Your stake completion history is saved — future updates will surface achievements and meta-unlocks based on how many of each stake type you've completed. Stake bonus items now safely fall back to stash if your bag is full.
implementationNotes: claimStake now calls scheduleSave() — previously the stake claim was not persisted until next other save event
stakesCompleted uses StakeId as key ('slayer' | 'scavenger' | 'deepdiver') but typed as Record<string, number> for forward-compatibility

publicInterface.name: CareerStats.stakesCompleted
publicInterface.description: Counts how many times each stake type was successfully completed across all runs.

codeSnippets.label: stakesCompleted increment in claimStake

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Stakes tracking + bag-full delivery fallback implemented this session
